art_highlights: Global art market recovered in 2025, growing 4% to $59.6B; high-end segment ($1M+) remains strong with 21% growth.
U.S. market holds 44% global share ($26B), leading recovery efforts.
Ultra-high-end transactions (over $10M) increased by 30%, signaling resilience in the top tier.
Dealer confidence for 2026 is cautiously optimistic, with a notable shift toward local collector engagement.
Postwar and Contemporary art segment undergoing gradual correction, yet maintaining 45% market share.

market_summary.market_sentiment: Cautious optimism; geopolitical tensions impacting sentiment.
market_summary.key_drivers: Middle East tensions, rising energy costs, mixed U.S. labor data, and central bank interest rate uncertainty.
market_summary.trend: Rotation away from mega-cap tech into Energy, Utilities, and Consumer Staples; small- and mid-cap companies showing strength.
